input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
## .NET MAUI Development Setup on macOS

### 1. Install Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | CLI compilers & headers |
| **.NET SDK (latest LTS or STS)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | .NET runtime + CLI |
| **MAUI workload** | `dotnet workload install maui` | MAUI templates & build targets |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically with MAUI workload, or [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) | Android build dependency |
| **Android SDK** | Installed automatically with MAUI workload, or via Android Studio | Android platform tools |

### 2. Step-by-Step

```bash
# 1. Accept Xcode license (after installing Xcode from App Store)
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
xcode-select --install

# 2. Install .NET SDK (use the installer pkg from dot.net/download)
#    For Apple Silicon, pick the Arm64 installer.

# 3. Install the MAUI workload (pulls Android SDK + OpenJDK automatically)
sudo dotnet workload install maui

# 4. (Optional) Install Android Studio for emulator management
#    https://developer.android.com/studio
```

### 3. Discover the Right Versions

```bash
# See available .NET SDK versions
dotnet --list-sdks

# See installed workloads and check for updates
dotnet workload list
dotnet workload update

# See which Android/iOS platforms the workload installed
dotnet workload search maui

# Check Xcode version (must meet minimum for your MAUI version)
xcodebuild -version
```

**Version compatibility** — always check the official matrix:
[learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/what-is-maui#supported-platforms](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms)

### 4. Verify Everything Works

```bash
# Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios      # adjust TFM to your SDK version

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run

# Run on Android Emulator (start emulator first via Android Studio)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

### 5. Optional but Recommended

- **Visual Studio Code** + [C# Dev Kit](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.csdevkit) + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) — lightweight IDE experience
- **Android Studio** — easier emulator/AVD management than CLI alone
- **Hot Reload** — built into `dotnet`, works with `dotnet watch` or the VS Code extension

### Key Gotchas

1. **Apple Silicon** — use the Arm64 .NET installer; Xcode is universal.
2. **Xcode minimum version** — each MAUI release has a minimum Xcode requirement (check release notes).
3. **`sudo` for workloads** — on macOS, `dotnet workload install` typically needs `sudo`.
4. **iOS device deployment** — requires an Apple Developer account and provisioning profile.
5. **Android SDK licenses** — accept them if prompted: `dotnet android sdk accept-licenses` or via Android Studio.
